data.description:
## Summary
_has_rebar_id_label (binding_walk.py) matches ANY label with the rebar-id:/rebar-id- PREFIX, including a malformed empty 'rebar-id:' (no local id). The L10 adopt guard then stands down (treats it as identity-bound) and defers to binding-recovery — but with an empty id there is nothing to re-link, so the key is never adopted AND never bound, and bridge-fsck reports it as unbound_jira on every run. Surfaced by REB-659 (a manual 'assignee clobber' experiment carrying label 'rebar-id:'), which produced a perpetual bare-forge-tapir drift alert.

## Reproduction Steps
1. A Jira issue has label 'rebar-id:' (empty value) and no binding-store entry.
2. Run the binding walk / bridge-fsck.
3. The issue is neither adopted (L10 stands down) nor bound → perpetual unbound_jira drift.

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: an empty/malformed rebar-id: marker is treated as UNMARKED (adoptable), so the key adopts (create+bind) normally.
- Actual: the empty marker counts as a binding marker → adoption stands down → the key is stranded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] _has_rebar_id_label returns True only for a WELL-FORMED marker (non-empty id after the prefix).
- [ ] A key with an empty 'rebar-id:' label is adopted (inbound create); a well-formed 'rebar-id:<id>' still stands down (no double-bind).
- [ ] Regression test in tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_binding_walk.py; reconciler suite green.
data.title: reconciler: malformed empty rebar-id: marker traps a Jira issue in unbound-drift limbo
